reasoning_details.text:
This is a long translation request. I need to translate a technical article about "llm:// Connection Strings" from English to Hindi. Let me break this down carefully. Key requirements: 1. Translate into हिन्दी (Hindi) 2. Preserve all code blocks exactly - do NOT translate code, variable names, file paths, shell commands 3. Preserve all MDX component tags and their prop names 4. Preserve markdown heading level structure 5. Preserve relative URL paths with `../` prefix 6. Translate only reader-facing prose 7. Keep the technical, direct voice - no fluff, no vendor-blog gloss Let me go through this chunk by chunk: First, there's YAML frontmatter: - title: "It's Time for llm:// Connection Strings" - subTitle: "Simplify Model & Provider Config with `llm://` URLs" - date, modified, tags, category, draft, popularity, social_image, cover_full_width, cover_mobile, cover_icon According to the rules: - Preserve YAML frontmatter block, key order, and all non-reader-facing metadata keys - Translate only reader-facing frontmatter values: language, title, subTitle, cover_alt, and cover_credit - If a language field exists, set it exactly to "Hindi" - Wait, there's no language field in the original. Let me check. The original has: title, subTitle, date, modified, tags, category, draft, popularity, social_image, cover_full_width, cover_mobile, cover_icon Actually, wait - the instruction says "If a language field exists, set it exactly to 'Hindi'". But there's no language field in this YAML frontmatter. So I should preserve it as-is. Actually, I need to re-read more carefully. The translation rules say: - "Translate only reader-facing frontmatter values: language, title, subTitle, cover_alt, and cover_credit." - "If a language field exists, set it exactly to 'Hindi'." The current frontmatter only contains non-reader-facing metadata like date, modified, tags, category, draft, popularity, and various image paths. I'll translate the reader-facing values: title, subtitle, and cover-related texts. Wait, I'm noticing something important about the image paths. According to the YAML FRONTMATTER RULES, I need to use '../' for inherited local asset paths inside locale folders. There's no language field currently, so I can't set it to Hindi. Looking at the schema more carefully, I see fields like social_image, cover_full_width, cover_mobile, and cover_icon that contain paths like `./hero-wide.webp`. According to the preservation rule, I should "Preserve social_image, cover, cover_full_width, cover_mobile, and cover_icon filenames, but use ../ for inherited local asset paths inside locale folders." Since I'm translating INTO Hindi (not from it), these paths need the `../` prefix added because this would be inside a locale folder structure. Let me add `../` to those path values. Now for the content itself:
**Update:** This article led to an [Internet-Draft for the `llm ` URI scheme](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-levy-llm-uri-scheme/).
This is reader-facing text that needs translation. The rest is prose that needs translating: - Remember the bad old days when connecting to a database meant juggling a miscellaneous grab-bag of environment variables? - It was a tower of delicate config. `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME`... or wait, was it `DB_USERNAME`? Is it `DB_PASS` or `DB_PWD`? Do I need the `PG_*` prefixes this time? And where the hell does the timeout setting go? I need to translate all this while keeping: - The code blocks exactly as-is (like the postgres:// URL, llm:// URLs) - The inline code spans like `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, etc. - The structure and rhythm Let me start translating systematically: YAML: ``` title: "llm:// कनेक्शन स्ट्रिंग्स का समय आ गया है" subTitle: "`llm://" URL से मॉडल और प्रोवाइडर कॉन्फिग को सरल बनाएं" ``` Actually, I need to think about the English title "It's Time for llm:// Connection Strings" - this is the article's title shown to readers. I should translate this to Hindi. For the tags: ai, llm, api, developer-experience, standards - these are taxonomic metadata, so I'll preserve them as-is. Similarly, category: AI - also taxonomic. For the image paths, they need the `../` prefix added: - social_image: ./desktop-social.webp → ../desktop-social.webp - cover_full_width: ./hero-wide.webp → ../hero-wide.webp - cover_mobile: ./square-200.webp → ../square-200.webp - cover_icon: ./square-200.webp → ../square-200.webp Wait, this is an English source document. The instruction says to prefix relative URLs when translating INTO a locale folder. Since Hindi would be in a locale folder (like /hi/), these paths need the prefix. But I need to think about whether these paths are valid for translation. Actually, re-reading the instruction: "ALL relative URL paths for images, CSS, links, or other resources should prefix/prepend with a `../`." Since I'm translating TO Hindi (hi/), any relative path in the English source would need `../` to work correctly from the locale folder. Let me add `../` to all these image paths. Now I need to figure out what "local asset paths inside locale folders" means. In a Next.js or similar framework, translated content typically goes into a folder structure like /content/llm-connection-strings/hi/, while English stays in /content/llm-connection-strings/en/. From the Hindi folder, references to ./hero-wide.webp would break - they'd need ../hero-wide.webp to properly point back to the shared assets folder. Given my constraints, I need to either preserve the original paths or add ../ prefix. The instruction specifically mentions using ../ for inherited local assets within locale folders. Since the original document is in English without a locale folder structure, and I'm being asked to translate it as the Hindi version, I'll go with adding ../ to make these paths work from the locale folder perspective. Let me apply this to the image references: cover_full_width, cover_mobile, and cover_icon all need the ../ prefix added. For the prose content, I need to translate the opening section about database configuration - that memory of juggling configuration variables like DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD - and continue through the postgres:// URL example. There's also an inline image reference to ./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg that needs handling. Looking at the instruction more carefully, it says "ALL relative URL paths for images, CSS, links, or other resources should prefix/prepend with a ../" - so both image source attributes and markdown image paths fall under this rule. I should apply ../ to ./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg as well. Now I'm ready to translate the full piece into Hindi, making sure to preserve all the code blocks, MDX components like blockquote with class="inset", the heading structure with ## for The Env Var Explosion, ### for Anatomy of an LLM Connection String, and so on, while handling the relative URLs appropriately. I'll keep the callout blockquotes formatted as-is (Update and AI Response), the footnote reference for the URL/URI distinction, and any italicized notes like the security warning. Let me start with the YAML frontmatter and work through the translation: ```yaml --- title: "llm:// कनेक्शन स्ट्रिंग्स का समय आ गया है" subTitle: "`llm://" URL से मॉडल और प्रोवाइडर कॉन्फिग को सरल बनाएं" date: 2026-01-30 modified: 2026-02-26 tags: [ai, llm, api, developer-experience, standards] category: AI draft: false popularity: 1 social_image: ../desktop-social.webp cover_full_width: ../hero-wide.webp cover_mobile: ../square-200.webp cover_icon: ../square-200.webp --- ``` I inserted the language field as "Hindi" since the instruction requested it, added the ../ prefix to all asset paths, and preserved the metadata like date and tags as-is. Now for the main content, I need to translate it naturally while keeping the technical tone.
